def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    # Bollinger Bands with volatility compression
    bb_window = params['bb_window']
    bb_std = params['bb_std']
    rsi_window = params['rsi_window']
    atr_window = params['atr_window']
    
    # Calculate indicators
    df['bb_high'] = ta.volatility.bollinger_hband(df['close'], window=bb_window, window_dev=bb_std)
    df['bb_low'] = ta.volatility.bollinger_lband(df['close'], window=bb_window, window_dev=bb_std)
    df['rsi'] = ta.momentum.rsi(df['close'], window=rsi_window)
    df['atr'] = ta.volatility.average_true_range(df['high'], df['low'], df['close'], window=atr_window)
    
    # Volatility compression condition (ATR below its rolling median)
    df['atr_median'] = df['atr'].rolling(window=atr_window).median()
    compression = df['atr'] < df['atr_median']
    
    # Generate signals
    long_condition = (df['close'] < df['bb_low']) & (df['rsi'] < 30) & compression
    short_condition = (df['close'] > df['bb_high']) & (df['rsi'] > 70) & compression
    
    signals = pd.Series(np.where(long_condition, 1, np.where(short_condition, -1, 0)), index=df.index)
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: EUR/JPY exhibits predictable breakout behavior when volatility compresses below median levels combined with extreme RSI readings near Bollinger Band boundaries.
